title: Bridge Essay: Paracingulate Sulcus Morphology and Hallucinations in Clinical and Nonclinical G

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Voice-hearing — auditory verbal hallucinations on acontinuum, inner speech misattribution, source monitoring, corollary discharge.

**Paracingulate Sulcus Morphology and Hallucinations in Clinical and Nonclinical Groups.**. Schizophr Bull.. PMID: 30380115. DOI: 10.1093/schbul/sby157. Hallucinations are acharacteristic symptom of psychotic mental health conditions that are also experienced by many individuals without aclinical diagnosis. Hallucinations in schizophrenia have been linked to differences in the length of the paracingulate sulcus (PCS), astructure in the medial prefrontal cortex which has previously been associated with the ability to differentiate perceived and imagined information.
